query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memslides: a hierarchical memory driven agent framework for personaliz" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (LLM-based personalization via memory management) and measures the same dependent variable (personalization performance) on the same domain (NLP systems), even though it proposes a "summary-augmented" alternative to the "hierarchical memory" approach in the user's specific follow-up question. It provides the necessary empirical baseline and methodological context for a literature review on extending memory-drive
